narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. H4 USD acceleration because CSM flips USD from weakest on D1 to strongest on H4 at 100, overriding bearish D1 read and driving broad dollar bid intraday.
2. Hot US CPI driving hawkish Fed repricing because it aligns with rising US 10Y yields at 4.463 up 1.2% and DXY gaining 0.36%, confirming rates-driven dollar demand.
3. CHF fading from D1 strength of 100 to H4 weakness of 0 because the dominant risk-on H4 regime is unwinding safe haven positioning, making CHF the clearest directional loser on the shorter timeframe.

PRICE CONFIRMS

USDCHF is the most structurally confirmed trade given its negative 0.87 correlation with EURUSD and negative 0.89 correlation with NZDUSD, meaning the broad dollar bid and CHF fade are reinforcing each other simultaneously from both sides. NZDUSD and AUDUSD are accelerating in CSM terms on H4, which conflicts with USD strength, suggesting these pairs are lagging the dollar move and will face downward pressure once the commodity rally in copper and oil stops providing offset. EURUSD H4 neutral signal despite EUR CSM accelerating to 66 indicates EUR is not yet responding to the dollar bid, confirming it is a laggard rather than a leader.

REGIME

Mixed, conflicted. VIX falling 2.12% to 17.99 and copper surging 3.52% argue risk-on, but S&P 500 is marginally negative and gold at 4723 continues to hold elevated, preventing a clean risk-on confirmation.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. USD CSM: H4 USD at 100 strongest contradicts D1 USD at 10 weakest, creating timeframe regime conflict that makes medium-term dollar longs vulnerable to reversal.
2. Commodity FX vs USD: Copper up 3.52% and oil up 4.19% should support AUD and NZD higher yet H4 USD dominance at 100 is pressing against those pairs simultaneously, creating an unresolved tug of war in AUDUSD and NZDUSD.

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF up - CHF fading from 100 to 0 CSM while USD surges from 10 to 100 - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDJPY up - H4 Buy signal with NZD accelerating CSM and JPY cross momentum confirmed at 0.90 correlation with AUDJPY - Conviction 6/10
Avoid: EURUSD - H4 neutral signal despite dual CSM acceleration makes direction indeterminate
Watch: DXY breaking above 99.00 confirms USD H4 regime dominance and validates USDCHF long; rejection there invalidates the primary trade.

themes.theme: Hot US CPI reignites inflation fears and hawkish Fed expectations
Rising crude oil prices on Hormuz closure and Iran ceasefire doubts
Equity market weakness and risk-off sentiment
Fed maintains neutral stance despite inflation concerns
European growth concerns and US tariff risks
Gold and precious metals under pressure from strong dollar and rising yields
GBP weakness with bearish technical signals emerging

key_observation: Hot CPI print is the dominant driver across markets, pushing USD higher, Treasury yields up, equities down, and commodities broadly weaker in nominal terms. Geopolitical tensions at Hormuz support energy prices but overall risk-off tone favors safe-haven dollar strength. Fed maintains measured communication despite hawkish inflation data.
watch: GBP/USD technical breakdown at 1.3500, ECB communications on persistent inflation, crude oil closure duration at Hormuz, equity market support levels, and any Fed member signals on rate path adjustment
